old_column.content: Unless noted, the options in this section are applied to all dev, build, and preview.

new_column.content: Unless noted, the options in this section are applied to all dev, build, and preview.

old_column.content: Project root directory (where `index.html` is located). Can be an absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory.

new_column.content: Project root directory (where `index.html` is located). Can be an absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory.

old_column.content: Base public path when served in development or production. Valid values include:

new_column.content: Base public path when served in development or production. Valid values include:

old_column.content: - Full URL, e.g. `https://bar.com/foo/` (The origin part won't be used in development so the value is the same as `/foo/`)

new_column.content: - Full URL, e.g. `https://bar.com/foo/` (The origin part won't be used in development so the value is the same as `/foo/`)

old_column.content: See [Public Base Path](/guide/build#public-base-path) for more details.

new_column.content: See [Public Base Path](/guide/build#public-base-path) for more details.

old_column.content: Specifying this in config will override the default mode for **both serve and build**. This value can also be overridden via the command line `--mode` option.

new_column.content: Specifying this in config will override the default mode for **both serve and build**. This value can also be overridden via the command line `--mode` option.

old_column.content: See [Env Variables and Modes](/guide/env-and-mode) for more details.

new_column.content: See [Env Variables and Modes](/guide/env-and-mode) for more details.

old_column.content: Define global constant replacements. Entries will be defined as globals during dev and statically replaced during build.

new_column.content: Define global constant replacements. Entries will be defined as globals during dev and statically replaced during build.

old_column.content: Vite uses [Oxc's define feature](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/transformer/global-variable-replacement#define) to perform replacements, so value expressions must be a string that contains a JSON-serializable value (null, boolean, number, string, array, or object) or a single identifier. For non-string values, Vite will automatically convert it to a string with `JSON.stringify`.

new_column.content: Vite uses [Oxc's define feature](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/transformer/global-variable-replacement#define) to perform replacements, so value expressions must be a string that contains a JSON-serializable value (null, boolean, number, string, array, or object) or a single identifier. For non-string values, Vite will automatically convert it to a string with `JSON.stringify`.

old_column.content: For TypeScript users, make sure to add the type declarations in the `vite-env.d.ts` file to get type checks and Intellisense.

new_column.content: For TypeScript users, make sure to add the type declarations in the `vite-env.d.ts` file to get type checks and Intellisense.

old_column.content: Array of plugins to use. Falsy plugins are ignored and arrays of plugins are flattened. If a promise is returned, it would be resolved before running. See [Plugin API](/guide/api-plugin) for more details on Vite plugins.

new_column.content: Array of plugins to use. Falsy plugins are ignored and arrays of plugins are flattened. If a promise is returned, it would be resolved before running. See [Plugin API](/guide/api-plugin) for more details on Vite plugins.

old_column.content: Directory to serve as plain static assets. Files in this directory are served at `/` during dev and copied to the root of `outDir` during build, and are always served or copied as-is without transform. The value can be either an absolute file system path or a path relative to project root.

new_column.content: Directory to serve as plain static assets. Files in this directory are served at `/` during dev and copied to the root of `outDir` during build, and are always served or copied as-is without transform. The value can be either an absolute file system path or a path relative to project root.

old_column.content: Directory to save cache files. Files in this directory are pre-bundled deps or some other cache files generated by vite, which can improve the performance. You can use `--force` flag or manually delete the directory to regenerate the cache files. The value can be either an absolute file system path or a path relative to project root. Default to `.vite` when no package.json is detected.

new_column.content: Directory to save cache files. Files in this directory are pre-bundled deps or some other cache files generated by vite, which can improve the performance. You can use `--force` flag or manually delete the directory to regenerate the cache files. The value can be either an absolute file system path or a path relative to project root. Default to `.vite` when no `package.json` is detected.

old_column.content: `Record<string, string> | Array<{ find: string | RegExp, replacement: string, customResolver?: ResolverFunction | ResolverObject }>`

new_column.content: `Record<string, string> | Array<{ find: string | RegExp, replacement: string, customResolver?: ResolverFunction | ResolverObject }>`

old_column.content: Defines aliases used to replace values in `import` or `require` statements. This works similar to [`@rollup/plugin-alias`](https://github.com/rollup/plugins/tree/master/packages/alias).

new_column.content: Defines aliases used to replace values in `import` or `require` statements. This works similar to [`@rollup/plugin-alias`](https://github.com/rollup/plugins/tree/master/packages/alias).

old_column.content: The order of the entries is important, in that the first defined rules are applied first.

new_column.content: The order of the entries is important, in that the first defined rules are applied first.

old_column.content: When aliasing to file system paths, always use absolute paths. Relative alias values will be used as-is and will not be resolved into file system paths.

new_column.content: When aliasing to file system paths, always use absolute paths. Relative alias values will be used as-is and will not be resolved into file system paths.

old_column.content: More advanced custom resolution can be achieved through [plugins](/guide/api-plugin).

new_column.content: More advanced custom resolution can be achieved through [plugins](/guide/api-plugin).

old_column.content: If you have configured aliases for [SSR externalized dependencies](/guide/ssr.md#ssr-externals), you may want to alias the actual `node_modules` packages. Both [Yarn](https://classic.yarnpkg.com/en/docs/cli/add/#toc-yarn-add-alias) and [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/aliases/) support aliasing via the `npm:` prefix.

new_column.content: If you have configured aliases for [SSR externalized dependencies](/guide/ssr.md#ssr-externals), you may want to alias the actual `node_modules` packages. Both [Yarn](https://classic.yarnpkg.com/en/docs/cli/add/#toc-yarn-add-alias) and [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/aliases/) support aliasing via the `npm:` prefix.

old_column.content: The Object format allows specifying aliases as a key, and the corresponding value as the actual import value. For example:

new_column.content: The Object format allows specifying aliases as a key, and the corresponding value as the actual import value. For example:

old_column.content: ### Array Format (`Array<{ find: string | RegExp, replacement: string, customResolver?: ResolverFunction | ResolverObject }>`)

new_column.content: ### Array Format (`Array<{ find: string | RegExp, replacement: string, customResolver?: ResolverFunction | ResolverObject }>`)

old_column.content: The Array format allows specifying aliases as objects, which can be useful for complex key/value pairs.

new_column.content: The Array format allows specifying aliases as objects, which can be useful for complex key/value pairs.

old_column.content: When `find` is a regular expression, the `replacement` can use [replacement patterns](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String/replace#specifying_a_string_as_the_replacement), such as `$1`. For example, to remove extensions with another, a pattern like the following might be used:

new_column.content: When `find` is a regular expression, the `replacement` can use [replacement patterns](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String/replace#specifying_a_string_as_the_replacement), such as `$1`. For example, to remove extensions with another, a pattern like the following might be used:

old_column.content: `customResolver` option can be used to provide separate module resolution for an individual alias.

new_column.content: `customResolver` option can be used to provide separate module resolution for an individual alias.

old_column.content: If you have duplicated copies of the same dependency in your app (likely due to hoisting or linked packages in monorepos), use this option to force Vite to always resolve listed dependencies to the same copy (from project root).

new_column.content: If you have duplicated copies of the same dependency in your app (likely due to hoisting or linked packages in monorepos), use this option to force Vite to always resolve listed dependencies to the same copy (from project root).

old_column.content: For SSR builds, deduplication does not work for ESM build outputs configured from `build.rollupOptions.output`. A workaround is to use CJS build outputs until ESM has better plugin support for module loading.

new_column.content: For SSR builds, deduplication does not work for ESM build outputs configured from `build.rollupOptions.output`. A workaround is to use CJS build outputs until ESM has better plugin support for module loading.

old_column.content: Additional allowed conditions when resolving [Conditional Exports](https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#packages_conditional_exports) from a package.

new_column.content: Additional allowed conditions when resolving [Conditional Exports](https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#packages_conditional_exports) from a package.

old_column.content: A package with conditional exports may have the following `exports` field in its `package.json`:

new_column.content: A package with conditional exports may have the following `exports` field in its `package.json`:

old_column.content: Here, `import` and `require` are "conditions". Conditions can be nested and should be specified from most specific to least specific.

new_column.content: Here, `import` and `require` are "conditions". Conditions can be nested and should be specified from most specific to least specific.

old_column.content: `development|production` is a special value that is replaced with `production` or `development` depending on the value of `process.env.NODE_ENV`. It is replaced with `production` when `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'` and `development` otherwise.

new_column.content: `development|production` is a special value that is replaced with `production` or `development` depending on the value of `process.env.NODE_ENV`. It is replaced with `production` when `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'` and `development` otherwise.

old_column.content: Note that `import`, `require`, `default` conditions are always applied if the requirements are met.

new_column.content: Note that `import`, `require`, `default` conditions are always applied if the requirements are met.

old_column.content: List of fields in `package.json` to try when resolving a package's entry point. Note this takes lower precedence than conditional exports resolved from the `exports` field: if an entry point is successfully resolved from `exports`, the main field will be ignored.

new_column.content: List of fields in `package.json` to try when resolving a package's entry point. Note this takes lower precedence than conditional exports resolved from the `exports` field: if an entry point is successfully resolved from `exports`, the main field will be ignored.

old_column.content: List of file extensions to try for imports that omit extensions. Note it is **NOT** recommended to omit extensions for custom import types (e.g. `.vue`) since it can interfere with IDE and type support.

new_column.content: List of file extensions to try for imports that omit extensions. Note it is **NOT** recommended to omit extensions for custom import types (e.g. `.vue`) since it can interfere with IDE and type support.

old_column.content: Enabling this setting causes vite to determine file identity by the original file path (i.e. the path without following symlinks) instead of the real file path (i.e. the path after following symlinks).

new_column.content: Enabling this setting causes vite to determine file identity by the original file path (i.e. the path without following symlinks) instead of the real file path (i.e. the path after following symlinks).

old_column.content: Enables the tsconfig paths resolution feature. `paths` option in `tsconfig.json` will be used to resolve imports. See [Features](/guide/features.md#paths) for more details.

new_column.content: Enables the tsconfig paths resolution feature. `paths` option in `tsconfig.json` will be used to resolve imports. See [Features](/guide/features.md#paths) for more details.

old_column.content: A nonce value placeholder that will be used when generating script / style tags. Setting this value will also generate a meta tag with nonce value.

new_column.content: A nonce value placeholder that will be used when generating script / style tags. Setting this value will also generate a meta tag with nonce value.

old_column.content: | ((name: string, filename: string, css: string) => string)

new_column.content: | ((name: string, filename: string, css: string) => string)

old_column.content: Configure CSS modules behavior. The options are passed on to [postcss-modules](https://github.com/css-modules/postcss-modules).

new_column.content: Configure CSS modules behavior. The options are passed on to [postcss-modules](https://github.com/css-modules/postcss-modules).

old_column.content: This option doesn't have any effect when using [Lightning CSS](../guide/features.md#lightning-css). If enabled, [`css.lightningcss.cssModules`](https://lightningcss.dev/css-modules.html) should be used instead.

new_column.content: This option doesn't have any effect when using [Lightning CSS](../guide/features.md#lightning-css). If enabled, [`css.lightningcss.cssModules`](https://lightningcss.dev/css-modules.html) should be used instead.

old_column.content: Inline PostCSS config or a custom directory to search PostCSS config from (default is project root).

new_column.content: Inline PostCSS config or a custom directory to search PostCSS config from (default is project root).

old_column.content: For inline PostCSS config, it expects the same format as `postcss.config.js`. But for `plugins` property, only [array format](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-load-config/blob/main/README.md#array) can be used.

new_column.content: For inline PostCSS config, it expects the same format as `postcss.config.js`. But for `plugins` property, only [array format](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-load-config/blob/main/README.md#array) can be used.

old_column.content: The search is done using [postcss-load-config](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-load-config) and only the supported config file names are loaded. Config files outside the workspace root (or the [project root](/guide/#index-html-and-project-root) if no workspace is found) are not searched by default. You can specify a custom path outside of the root to load the specific config file instead if needed.

new_column.content: The search is done using [postcss-load-config](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-load-config) and only the supported config file names are loaded. Config files outside the workspace root (or the [project root](/guide/#index-html-and-project-root) if no workspace is found) are not searched by default. You can specify a custom path outside of the root to load the specific config file instead if needed.

old_column.content: Note if an inline config is provided, Vite will not search for other PostCSS config sources.

new_column.content: Note if an inline config is provided, Vite will not search for other PostCSS config sources.

old_column.content: Specify options to pass to CSS pre-processors. The file extensions are used as keys for the options. The supported options for each preprocessor can be found in their respective documentation:

new_column.content: Specify options to pass to CSS pre-processors. The file extensions are used as keys for the options. The supported options for each preprocessor can be found in their respective documentation:

old_column.content: - Uses `sass-embedded` if installed, otherwise uses `sass`. For the best performance, it's recommended to install the `sass-embedded` package.

new_column.content: - Uses `sass-embedded` if installed, otherwise uses `sass`. For the best performance, it's recommended to install the `sass-embedded` package.

old_column.content: - `styl`/`stylus`: Only [`define`](https://stylus-lang.com/docs/js.html#define-name-node) is supported, which can be passed as an object.

new_column.content: - `styl`/`stylus`: Only [`define`](https://stylus-lang.com/docs/js.html#define-name-node) is supported, which can be passed as an object.

old_column.content: - **Type:** `string | ((source: string, filename: string) => (string | { content: string; map?: SourceMap }))`

new_column.content: - **Type:** `string | ((source: string, filename: string) => (string | { content: string; map?: SourceMap }))`

old_column.content: This option can be used to inject extra code for each style content. Note that if you include actual styles and not just variables, those styles will be duplicated in the final bundle.

new_column.content: This option can be used to inject extra code for each style content. Note that if you include actual styles and not just variables, those styles will be duplicated in the final bundle.

old_column.content: Since the same code is prepended to files in different directories, relative paths won't resolve correctly. Use absolute paths or [aliases](#resolve-alias) instead.

new_column.content: Since the same code is prepended to files in different directories, relative paths won't resolve correctly. Use absolute paths or [aliases](#resolve-alias) instead.

old_column.content: Specifies the maximum number of threads CSS preprocessors can use. `true` means up to the number of CPUs minus 1. When set to `0`, Vite will not create any workers and will run the preprocessors in the main thread.

new_column.content: Specifies the maximum number of threads CSS preprocessors can use. `true` means up to the number of CPUs minus 1. When set to `0`, Vite will not create any workers and will run the preprocessors in the main thread.

old_column.content: Depending on the preprocessor options, Vite may run the preprocessors on the main thread even if this option is not set to `0`.

new_column.content: Depending on the preprocessor options, Vite may run the preprocessors on the main thread even if this option is not set to `0`.

old_column.content: Whether to enable sourcemaps during dev.

new_column.content: Whether to enable sourcemaps during dev.

old_column.content: Selects the engine used for CSS processing. Check out [Lightning CSS](../guide/features.md#lightning-css) for more information.

new_column.content: Selects the engine used for CSS processing. Check out [Lightning CSS](../guide/features.md#lightning-css) for more information.

old_column.content: Note that postcss (postcss-import) has a different behavior with duplicated `@import` from browsers. See [postcss/postcss-import#462](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-import/issues/462).

new_column.content: Note that postcss (postcss-import) has a different behavior with duplicated `@import` from browsers. See [postcss/postcss-import#462](https://github.com/postcss/postcss-import/issues/462).

old_column.content: Configures Lightning CSS. Full transform options can be found in [the Lightning CSS repo](https://github.com/parcel-bundler/lightningcss/blob/master/node/index.d.ts).

new_column.content: Configures Lightning CSS. Full transform options can be found in [the Lightning CSS repo](https://github.com/parcel-bundler/lightningcss/blob/master/node/index.d.ts).

old_column.content: Whether to support named imports from `.json` files.

new_column.content: Whether to support named imports from `.json` files.

old_column.content: If set to `true`, imported JSON will be transformed into `export default JSON.parse("...")` which is significantly more performant than Object literals, especially when the JSON file is large.

new_column.content: If set to `true`, imported JSON will be transformed into `export default JSON.parse("...")` which is significantly more performant than Object literals, especially when the JSON file is large.

old_column.content: If set to `'auto'`, the data will be stringified only if [the data is bigger than 10kB](https://v8.dev/blog/cost-of-javascript-2019#json:~:text=A%20good%20rule%20of%20thumb%20is%20to%20apply%20this%20technique%20for%20objects%20of%2010%20kB%20or%20larger).

new_column.content: If set to `'auto'`, the data will be stringified only if [the data is bigger than 10kB](https://v8.dev/blog/cost-of-javascript-2019#json:~:text=A%20good%20rule%20of%20thumb%20is%20to%20apply%20this%20technique%20for%20objects%20of%2010%20kB%20or%20larger).

old_column.content: `OxcOptions` extends [Oxc Transformer's options](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/transformer). The most common use case is customizing JSX:

new_column.content: `OxcOptions` extends [Oxc Transformer's options](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/transformer). The most common use case is customizing JSX:

old_column.content: By default, transformation by Oxc is applied to `ts`, `jsx` and `tsx` files. You can customize this with `oxc.include` and `oxc.exclude`, which can be a regex, a [picomatch](https://github.com/micromatch/picomatch#globbing-features) pattern, or an array of either.

new_column.content: By default, transformation by Oxc is applied to `ts`, `jsx` and `tsx` files. You can customize this with `oxc.include` and `oxc.exclude`, which can be a regex, a [picomatch](https://github.com/micromatch/picomatch#globbing-features) pattern, or an array of either.

old_column.content: In addition, you can also use `oxc.jsxInject` to automatically inject JSX helper imports for every file transformed by Oxc:

new_column.content: In addition, you can also use `oxc.jsxInject` to automatically inject JSX helper imports for every file transformed by Oxc:

old_column.content: Set to `false` to disable transformation by Oxc.

new_column.content: Set to `false` to disable transformation by Oxc.

old_column.content: This option is converted to `oxc` option internally. Use `oxc` option instead.

new_column.content: This option is converted to `oxc` option internally. Use `oxc` option instead.

old_column.content: Specify additional [picomatch patterns](https://github.com/micromatch/picomatch#globbing-features) to be treated as static assets so that:

new_column.content: Specify additional [picomatch patterns](https://github.com/micromatch/picomatch#globbing-features) to be treated as static assets so that:

old_column.content: - They will be excluded from the plugin transform pipeline when referenced from HTML or directly requested over `fetch` or XHR.

new_column.content: - They will be excluded from the plugin transform pipeline when referenced from HTML or directly requested over `fetch` or XHR.

old_column.content: - Importing them from JS will return their resolved URL string (this can be overwritten if you have a `enforce: 'pre'` plugin to handle the asset type differently).

new_column.content: - Importing them from JS will return their resolved URL string (this can be overwritten if you have a `enforce: 'pre'` plugin to handle the asset type differently).

old_column.content: The built-in asset type list can be found [here](https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/vite/src/node/constants.ts).

new_column.content: The built-in asset type list can be found [here](https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/vite/src/node/constants.ts).

old_column.content: Adjust console output verbosity. Default is `'info'`.

new_column.content: Adjust console output verbosity. Default is `'info'`.

old_column.content: Use a custom logger to log messages. You can use Vite's `createLogger` API to get the default logger and customize it to, for example, change the message or filter out certain warnings.

new_column.content: Use a custom logger to log messages. You can use Vite's `createLogger` API to get the default logger and customize it to, for example, change the message or filter out certain warnings.

old_column.content: Set to `false` to prevent Vite from clearing the terminal screen when logging certain messages. Via command line, use `--clearScreen false`.

new_column.content: Set to `false` to prevent Vite from clearing the terminal screen when logging certain messages. Via command line, use `--clearScreen false`.

old_column.content: The directory from which `.env` files are loaded. Can be an absolute path, or a path relative to the project root. `false` will disable the `.env` file loading.

new_column.content: The directory from which `.env` files are loaded. Can be an absolute path, or a path relative to the project root. `false` will disable the `.env` file loading.

old_column.content: See [here](/guide/env-and-mode#env-files) for more about environment files.

new_column.content: See [here](/guide/env-and-mode#env-files) for more about environment files.

old_column.content: Env variables starting with `envPrefix` will be exposed to your client source code via `import.meta.env`.

new_column.content: Env variables starting with `envPrefix` will be exposed to your client source code via `import.meta.env`.

old_column.content: `envPrefix` should not be set as `''`, which will expose all your env variables and cause unexpected leaking of sensitive information. Vite will throw an error when detecting `''`.

new_column.content: `envPrefix` should not be set as `''`, which will expose all your env variables and cause unexpected leaking of sensitive information. Vite will throw an error when detecting `''`.

old_column.content: If you would like to expose an unprefixed variable, you can use [define](#define) to expose it:

new_column.content: If you would like to expose an unprefixed variable, you can use [define](#define) to expose it:

old_column.content: Whether your application is a Single Page Application (SPA), a [Multi Page Application (MPA)](../guide/build#multi-page-app), or Custom Application (SSR and frameworks with custom HTML handling):

new_column.content: Whether your application is a Single Page Application (SPA), a [Multi Page Application (MPA)](../guide/build#multi-page-app), or Custom Application (SSR and frameworks with custom HTML handling):

old_column.content: - `'spa'`: include HTML middlewares and use SPA fallback. Configure [sirv](https://github.com/lukeed/sirv) with `single: true` in preview

new_column.content: - `'spa'`: include HTML middlewares and use SPA fallback. Configure [sirv](https://github.com/lukeed/sirv) with `single: true` in preview

old_column.content: Learn more in Vite's [SSR guide](/guide/ssr#vite-cli). Related: [`server.middlewareMode`](./server-options#server-middlewaremode).

new_column.content: Learn more in Vite's [SSR guide](/guide/ssr#vite-cli). Related: [`server.middlewareMode`](./server-options#server-middlewaremode).

old_column.content: Enable future breaking changes to prepare for a smooth migration to the next major version of Vite. The list may be updated, added, or removed at any time as new features are developed.

new_column.content: Enable future breaking changes to prepare for a smooth migration to the next major version of Vite. The list may be updated, added, or removed at any time as new features are developed.

old_column.content: See the [Breaking Changes](/changes/) page for details of the possible options.

new_column.content: See the [Breaking Changes](/changes/) page for details of the possible options.
